q: How do you systematically benchmark a Genie Space's accuracy? Describe the test loop.
a: Databricks provides a first-class Benchmarks feature inside each Genie Space (requires CAN EDIT). The loop is: (1) Build a ground-truth corpus — for each high-value question you want Genie to answer correctly, write or verify the authoritative SQL and add it as a benchmark. The Benchmarks UI lets you add from chat responses, from the Monitoring tab's user questions, or by writing SQL from scratch. Cover 2-4 phrasings per question since Genie's accuracy varies with wording. (2) Run the benchmark — select all or a subset and click Run benchmarks. Runs are async so you can keep working. (3) Evaluate results — Chat mode compares Genie's result set against the ground-truth SQL's result set and rates each as Good (exact match, same data different sort, numeric values matching to 4 significant digits) or Bad (empty result, wrong columns, mismatched cells). Agent mode uses an LLM judge with your optional evaluation notes. Each Bad answer includes an LLM explanation of what went wrong. (4) Iterate — for each Bad answer, decide the intervention: a SQL expression for a misunderstood metric, an example query for a wrong join pattern, or an Instructions rule for a behavioral issue. Re-run the benchmark to confirm the fix didn't break other questions. (5) Promote improvements — if Genie's generated answer is now better than the stored ground truth, click Update ground truth to advance the baseline. The monitoring tab's weekly digest and user feedback upvotes are leading indicators between benchmark runs.
fj: I test Genie by asking a few questions myself and seeing if the answers look right.
fs: Manual spot checks don't catch regressions when you add new SQL expressions or Instructions. The benchmark loop (ground-truth SQL corpus → async run → Good/Bad LLM grading → targeted intervention → re-run) gives a repeatable accuracy signal you can track over time.
